item.title: Leafs' Auston Matthews Out for Season After Knee Injury
item.description: Toronto Maple Leafs captain Auston Matthews will miss the remainder of the 2025-26 NHL season due to a knee injury sustained in a game against the Anaheim Ducks. Matthews will be re-evaluated in two weeks.

item.title: Cincinnati Fires Men's Basketball Coach Wes Miller After 5 Seasons
item.description: Cincinnati has fired men's basketball coach Wes Miller after five seasons, citing the team's inability to reach the NCAA tournament. Miller's contract states that he is owed $9.9 million if fired before March 31, 2026.

item.title: Titans Release CB L'Jarius Sneed, Clear $11.4M in Cap Space
item.description: The Tennessee Titans are releasing cornerback L'Jarius Sneed, saving $11.4 million in cap space. Sneed struggled with injuries during his time with the team.

item.title: 2026 NFL Free Agency Live Updates: Signings, Trades, Cuts
item.description: The 2026 NFL free agency period has begun, with several key signings and trades already taking place. The Baltimore Ravens have agreed to terms with edge rusher Trey Hendrickson, while quarterback Malik Willis is joining the Miami Dolphins and Tua Tagovailoa is signing with the Atlanta Falcons.

item.title: Tewkesbury Mother Sentenced to 13 Years for Enslaving Woman for 25 Years
item.description: A mother-of-ten has been sentenced to 13 years in prison for enslaving a vulnerable woman for 25 years in Tewkesbury, Gloucestershire. The victim suffered physical and emotional abuse, including being squirted with washing-up liquid and being forced to live off scraps.

item.title: G7 Supports Record Oil Reserve Release Amid Price Surge
item.description: G7 nations support the release of oil from their reserves to tackle soaring prices, with the International Energy Agency potentially releasing 300 to 400 million barrels of oil.

item.title: Teenager Arrested After Girl Stabbed at Norfolk School
item.description: A 16-year-old boy has been arrested after a teenage girl was stabbed at Thorpe St Andrew School in Norfolk, with the students placed under lockdown conditions. The victim suffered minor injuries.

item.title: Couple Dies After Being Pulled from Water at Australian Beach
item.description: A married couple from the UK died after being pulled from the water at Shellharbour beach in New South Wales, Australia. Police praised the efforts of members of the public who attempted to save the couple.

item.title: Mortgage Rates Surge Amid Iran War Uncertainty
item.description: The UK mortgage market is experiencing its most intense turbulence since the mini-Budget of 2022, with mortgage rates rising and nearly 500 products being pulled from the market.

item.title: Global Music Trends: English No Longer Dominant in Charts, Says Spotify
item.description: According to Spotify, pop music's bias towards English is fading, with songs in 16 different languages appearing in its Global Top 50 last year. The company also reported a 6% increase in royalties paid to UK artists.

item.title: UK Government to Publish Mandelson Documents on Wednesday
item.description: The UK government will publish internal documents on Lord Mandelson's appointment as British ambassador to the US on Wednesday, following a parliamentary procedure to compel the release of the papers.

item.title: Russian Courier Speaks Out on Exploding Parcels Plot Against UK
item.description: A Russian courier has spoken out on his involvement in a plot to send exploding parcels to the UK and other countries, alleging he was hired to deliver the parcels without knowing their true contents.

item.title: Meta Acquires Moltbook, Social Media Platform for AI Bots
item.description: Meta has acquired Moltbook, a social media platform for AI bots, with plans to develop new ways for AI agents to work for people and businesses.

item.title: Iranian Football Delegation Member Reconsiders Australian Asylum
item.description: One member of the Iranian football delegation who initially accepted asylum in Australia has changed her mind, while the remaining players have left the country.

item.title: UK Government Bans Al Quds Day March Over Public Disorder Fears
item.description: The UK government has banned the annual Al Quds Day march in London due to concerns over public disorder, with a stationary demonstration to be held instead under strict conditions.

item.title: UK Weather: Met Office Issues Yellow Wind Warning Amid Snow and Cold Return
item.description: The Met Office has issued a yellow wind warning for the UK, with strengthening winds developing across the country and temperatures set to drop with a return to wintry weather.

item.title: Epstein's Aides Still Control His Money and Secrets, Despite Congressional Subpoena
item.description: Richard Kahn and Darren Indyke, the sole executors of Jeffrey Epstein's estate, are still controlling his wealth and possessions, despite being subpoenaed to testify before a congressional committee.

item.title: Insurgents Shake Up Vets Market with Affordable Options
item.description: Pet owners in the UK are struggling to afford rising vet bills, which have increased by 63% between 2016 and 2023. The Competition and Markets Authority has launched an investigation into the industry to address the issue.

item.title: Former Deputy PM Clegg Criticizes University Tuition Fee System
item.description: Former Deputy PM Sir Nick Clegg has criticized the university tuition fee system, describing it as "deeply unfair" due to the repeated freezing of graduate repayment thresholds.

item.title: Behind the Scenes of OnlyFans: Life as a Low-Paid 'Chatter'
item.description: A Philippines-based woman has spoken out about the harsh realities of working as a low-paid 'chatter' on the online platform OnlyFans, describing the experience as 'heartbreaking' and 'icky'.

item.title: Last Hereditary Peers to Leave House of Lords
item.description: The House of Lords (Hereditary Peers) Bill has passed, paving the way for the removal of the last remaining 92 hereditary peers from the upper house.

item.title: Meta Urged to Boost Oversight of Fake AI Videos
item.description: Meta's Oversight Board has called on the company to enhance its oversight of fake AI content, citing concerns about the proliferation of such content on its platforms, particularly during armed conflicts.

item.title: Behind the Scenes of OnlyFans: The Unseen Labour of 'Chatters'
item.description: A hidden workforce of 'chatters' is behind the success of some OnlyFans models, while scientists use AI to advance their research and the White House is accused of manipulating footage for propaganda purposes.

item.title: Epic Raises Fortnite V-Bucks Price Amid Cost Increases
item.description: Epic Games has announced that it will be increasing the price of Fortnite's V-Bucks, citing rising costs as the reason. The price hike will take effect on March 19, and will see the cost of V-Bucks increase, while bonus currency rewards will be removed from the game's main battle pass.

item.title: Signal Issues Warning After Hackers Target Officials and Users
item.description: Signal has issued a warning to users after hackers targeted high-profile users, including officials and journalists, in a Russian-backed campaign. The company advises users to be vigilant against scams and not to share sensitive information.

item.title: Tumbler Ridge Shooting: Family Sues OpenAI Over Alleged Failure to Warn Authorities
item.description: The family of Maya Gebala, a 12-year-old girl injured in a Canadian school shooting, has sued OpenAI for allegedly failing to warn authorities about the suspect's plans.

item.title: FAA Briefly Grounds JetBlue Flights Due to System Outage
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration briefly grounded all JetBlue flights due to a system outage, which was resolved within 40 minutes. The airline did not provide further information on the cause of the outage.

item.title: GPS Jamming Threatens Middle East Maritime Navigation
item.description: GPS jamming in the Middle East is causing concern for maritime navigation, with hundreds of ships mysteriously clustered in the wrong locations in waters off Iran, the UAE, and Qatar.

item.title: Anthropic Sues US Government Over 'Supply Chain Risk' Label
item.description: Anthropic, an AI firm, sues the US government over claims it is a 'supply chain risk' and alleges the government's actions were 'unprecedented and unlawful'.

item.title: London Mayor Courts Embattled AI Firm Anthropic Amid US Tensions
item.description: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan invites embattled AI firm Anthropic to expand its operations in London, amid a dispute with the US government over access to its AI tools.
